tasks.description: Journey tab not in tab bar after creation from Compare view CTA — call refreshTabs() after createTab() to update workspace context before navigation
acceptance_criteria: After clicking 'Create Journey Tab' in Compare view, the new tab appears in the tab bar
After clicking 'Create Process Tab' in Compare view, the new tab appears in the tab bar
Navigation to the new tab's canvas view works correctly after creation
No regression in existing tab bar behavior for already-existing tabs

patterns_to_follow: useWorkspace() hook provides refreshTabs() — call it after createTab() succeeds, before router.push()
Other views that create entities and need context refresh use this same pattern

tasks.special_instructions: Import useWorkspace from @/lib/context/workspace-context. In both handleCreateJourneyTab and handleCreateProcessTab, add `await refreshTabs()` after the createTab() call and before router.push(). The refreshTabs() function fetches updated tabs from the API and sets them in context, so the tab bar (which reads from context) will show the new tab immediately.

tasks.description: Tools page heading hierarchy violation — h2 'Tools Summary' without h1. Change to h1 or add visually-hidden h1.
acceptance_criteria: Tools page has an h1 element as the first heading in the DOM
Heading hierarchy is logical (h1 before h2, no skipped levels)
Visual appearance is unchanged (use sr-only if adding a hidden h1)

patterns_to_follow: Other workspace views use a visually hidden h1 or the header component provides it. Check how other view pages handle heading hierarchy.
The summary sidebar h2 at line 540 should stay h2 — add a sr-only h1 'Tools' before the ReactFlow canvas area

tasks.special_instructions: Add a visually-hidden h1 element (className='sr-only') with text 'Tools' as the first child of the outermost container div. Keep the existing h2 'Tools Summary' as-is. The sr-only class is built into Tailwind.

tasks.description: Radix hydration mismatch on gap-analysis and tools pages — DropdownMenuTrigger ID offset between server/client
acceptance_criteria: No hydration mismatch console warnings on cold-navigate to /gap-analysis
No hydration mismatch console warnings on cold-navigate to /tools
User menu dropdown still functions correctly
No regressions on other pages that use DropdownMenu

patterns_to_follow: Radix generates incremental IDs during render. Server/client mismatch means different numbers of Radix components render. The gap-analysis and tools pages likely have client-only Radix components (e.g., Select, DropdownMenu in view components) that don't render on server.
Fix: add suppressHydrationWarning={true} to the DropdownMenuTrigger button in header.tsx, OR investigate why SSR component count differs
learnings_relevant: BUG-030 hydration fix pattern — similar date formatting hydration issue was resolved by consistent formatting

tasks.special_instructions: The root cause is that gap-analysis and tools pages render client-only Radix components that shift Radix's internal ID counter. The cleanest fix is to add `suppressHydrationWarning` to the DropdownMenuTrigger's `<button>` element in header.tsx. This is a known pattern for Radix ID mismatches in Next.js SSR. Alternative: if header.tsx DropdownMenu can be made client-only (dynamic import with ssr:false), that would also fix it. Prefer the simpler suppressHydrationWarning approach.

testing_plan.regression_reason: Regression ran 2 iterations ago (iter 120). Not due.

warnings_added: BUG-035 (P1) still blocked — Supabase CLI unauthenticated. Manual npx supabase login required.
BUG-032 same root cause as BUG-035 — blocked until migration 024 pushed.
